claim_bullets.bullet_text: The return statement is the first statement we've seen, and it behaves differently than an expression. (raw/javascriptallonge.pdf p.37-38)
Statements belong inside blocks and only inside blocks. (raw/javascriptallonge.pdf p.37-38)
For example, you can't use one as the expression in a simple function, because it isn't an expression: (() => return 0)() //=> ERROR (raw/javascriptallonge.pdf p.37-38)

source_record_text: This chapter discusses return statements and the distinction between statements and expressions in JavaScript.
